item.title: Discovering Descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
This talk was preﾂｭsentﾂｭed at PyﾂｭCon CZ 2017 and EuﾂｭroPyﾂｭthon 2017. Here are the refﾂｭerﾂｭences and reﾂｭsources for the preﾂｭsenﾂｭtaﾂｭtion.

The code used for the talk, is availﾂｭable here and ﾂｭconﾂｭtains some exﾂｭplaﾂｭnaﾂｭtions along with the exﾂｭamﾂｭples.

Read more窶ｦ (1 min remaining to read)

item.title: The __set_name__ method for descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Deﾂｭscripﾂｭtors genﾂｭerﾂｭalﾂｭly have to inﾂｭterﾂｭact with atﾂｭtributes of the manﾂｭaged obﾂｭjecﾂｭt, and this is done by inﾂｭspectﾂｭing __ﾂｭdicﾂｭt__ on that obﾂｭject (or callﾂｭing getatﾂｭtr/seﾂｭtatﾂｭtr, but the probﾂｭlem is the same), and findﾂｭing the key unﾂｭderﾂｭ the speﾂｭcifﾂｭic name.

For this reaﾂｭson, the deﾂｭscripﾂｭtor will have to know the name of the key to look ﾂｭfor, which is reﾂｭlatﾂｭed to the name of the atﾂｭtribute is manﾂｭagﾂｭing.

On preﾂｭviﾂｭous verﾂｭsions of Python this had to be done exﾂｭplicﾂｭitﾂｭly. If we wantﾂｭed to ﾂｭwork around it, there were some more adﾂｭvanced ways to do so. Luckﾂｭiﾂｭly, afﾂｭter PEP-487 (added in Python 3.6), there are some enﾂｭhanceﾂｭments reﾂｭgardﾂｭing class creﾂｭation, which alﾂｭso afﾂｭfectﾂｭs deﾂｭscripﾂｭtors.

Let's reﾂｭview the probﾂｭlem, the preﾂｭviﾂｭous apﾂｭproachﾂｭes to tackﾂｭle it, and the modﾂｭern way of solvﾂｭing it.

Read more窶ｦ (4 min remaining to read)

item.title: Descriptors & Decorators
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Deﾂｭscripﾂｭtors are an amazﾂｭing tool to have in our toolﾂｭbox, as they come in handy in many opﾂｭporﾂｭtuﾂｭniﾂｭties.

Probﾂｭaﾂｭbly the best thing about deﾂｭscripﾂｭtors, is that they can imﾂｭprove othﾂｭer ﾂｭsoﾂｭluﾂｭtionﾂｭs. Let's see how we can write betﾂｭter decﾂｭoﾂｭraﾂｭtors, by usﾂｭing deﾂｭscripﾂｭtors.

Read more窶ｦ (5 min remaining to read)

item.title: Types of Descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Reﾂｭsumﾂｭing from where we left ofﾂｭf, on the preﾂｭviﾂｭous post, on which we took A first look at deﾂｭscripﾂｭtors, it's time to exﾂｭplore their difﾂｭferﾂｭent typeﾂｭs and how they work inﾂｭterﾂｭnalﾂｭly.

In Python, almost everything is represented with a dictionary. Objects are dictionaries. Classes are objects, hence they also are contained into a dictionary. This is denoted by the __dict__ attribute that objects have.

There are two types of descriptors: data descriptors and non-data ones. If a descriptor implements both [1] __get__() and __set__(), it's called a data descriptor; otherwise is a non-data descriptor.

Read more窶ｦ (7 min remaining to read)

item.title: A first look at descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Deﾂｭscripﾂｭtors are one of the most powﾂｭerﾂｭful feaﾂｭtures of Python. The reaﾂｭson why they're so powﾂｭerﾂｭful is beﾂｭcause they enﾂｭable us to conﾂｭtrol the core opﾂｭerﾂｭaﾂｭtions (get, set, delete) [1], of an atﾂｭtribute in a givﾂｭen obﾂｭjecﾂｭt, so that we can hook a parﾂｭticﾂｭuﾂｭlar code, conﾂｭtrolled by us, in orﾂｭder to modﾂｭiﾂｭfy, change, or exﾂｭtend the oﾂｭrigﾂｭiﾂｭnal opﾂｭerﾂｭaﾂｭtion.

Read more窶ｦ (8 min remaining to read)
